title: rosa × collina var. cinerea
intro: Rosa × collina var. cinerea, also known as the Grey Wild Rose, is a variety of flowering shrub that belongs to the rose family and is native to certain regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The Grey Wild Rose is known to provide several health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, relieving menstrual cramps, and reducing fever. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
